libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Retain or regenerate the local test stubs for Android, Conscrypt, dalvik CloseGuard, and Jetty ALPN classes when exercising okhttp3.internal.platform reflective code. Keep the native-image test build argument -H:+AddAllCharsets because okhttp3.internal.Util eagerly resolves UTF-32 charsets during static initialization. The javac failure is from OkHttp API drift: in 3.8.1 Util.intersect takes a Comparator as its first argument, not Class<String>.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #6518 [Automation] javac compile fails for com.squareup.okhttp3:okhttp:3.8.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding mockwebserver is useful for upstream-style HTTP integration tests but is not required for the existing metadata-oriented coverage and would be unnecessary setup for this compile fix.
If the optional platform stubs are omitted, tests may still compile but will miss the Android, dalvik CloseGuard, Conscrypt, and Jetty ALPN reflective paths that the existing metadata covers.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: OkHttp 3.8.1 does not need an extra Maven dependency or Docker service; its meaningful metadata coverage depends on test-code/native-image setup for optional platform-reflection paths and charset initialization.
